sections.paragraph:
εὕρημα, ατος, τό (s. next entry; Trag., Hdt. et al.; POxy 472, 33 [II a.d.]; Philo; Jos., C. Ap. 2, 148; the LXX has the later form εὕρεμα [Phryn. p. 445f Lob.; Dio Chrys. 59 (76), 1]; s. Thackeray p. 80) discovery, invention Dg 7:1.—DELG s.v. εὑρίσκω.

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Phryn Phryn , the Atticist, signifying his Eclogae, II a.d.—List 5

Lob. Lob. = CLobeck, s. Phryn.—List 5

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Thackeray Thackeray = HT., A Grammar of the OT in Greek according to the Septuagint—List 6

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 411
